qtype: money and percentage calculations, reverse percentage
tex:
(a) In 2023 a football club had 50 adult members and 70 child members.
\newline

The membership fee for an adult was \$40 and the membership fee for a child was \$15.
\newline

(i) (b) Calculate the total of the membership fees received by the club in 2023.
\newline

(c) The cost of running the club in 2023 was \$2780.
\newline

Calculate \$2780 as a percentage of the total of the membership fees received by the club.
\newline

(d) In 2023 there were 120 members.
\newline

This was a decrease by 4\% of the number of members in 2022.
\newline

Calculate the number of members in 2022.
\newline

(e) In 2024 the total number of members increased from the 120 members in 2023.
\newline

The number of adult members and the number of child members each increased by the same number.
\newline

The ratio number of adult members : number of child members changed to 14 : 19.
\newline

\begin{subsubparts}

(f) Find the total number of members in 2024.
\newline

(g) Calculate the percentage increase in the total number of members from 2023 to 2024.
\newline

\end{subsubparts}

(h) The population of a village is 2500.
\newline

The population is decreasing exponentially at a rate of 3\% per year.
\newline

(i) (i) Calculate the population at the end of 3 years.
\newline

(j) Find the number of complete years it takes for the population to first fall below 2000.
\newline

content.content:

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

This was a decrease by 4\% of the number of members in 2022.
\newline

Calculate the number of members in 2022.
\newline

In 2024 the total number of members increased from the 120 members in 2023.
\newline

The number of adult members and the number of child members each increased by the same number.
\newline

The ratio number of adult members : number of child members changed to 14 : 19.
\newline
Find the total number of members in 2024.
Calculate the percentage increase in the total number of members from 2023 to 2024.

The population of a village is 2500.
\newline

The population is decreasing exponentially at a rate of 3\% per year.
\newline

Calculate the population at the end of 3 years.
\newline

Find the number of complete years it takes for the population to first fall below 2000.
\newline

qtype: finding terms of sequences, finding nth term from general formula, solving equations involving sequences
tex:

\newline

(i) (b) Find the 4th term of this sequence.
\newline

(c) Find the value of $n$ when the nth term is $-1211$.
\newline

(d) The nth term of a different sequence is $3 \times (0.2)^{n-1}$.
\newline

Find the 5th term of this sequence.
\newline

(e) The table shows the first four terms of sequences A, B and C.
\newline

[Diagram]
Complete the table for each sequence.
\newline

qtype: rates and average speed calculations, limits of accuracy, time conversion
tex:
(a) (i) (b) A car travels $50\,\text{km}$ at an average speed of $75\,\text{km/h}$.
\newline

Find the time taken.
\newline

Give your answer in minutes.
\newline

(c) Another car travels $47\,\text{km}$, correct to the nearest kilometre.
\newline

The average speed of this car is $75\,\text{km/h}$, correct to the nearest $5\,\text{km/h}$.
\newline

Calculate the lower bound of the time taken.
\newline

Give your answer in minutes.
\newline

(d) A train travels a total of $240\,\text{km}$.
\newline

The train travels for $t$ minutes at an average speed of $100\,\text{km/h}$.
\newline

It then travels for $(t + 60)$ minutes at an average speed of $110\,\text{km/h}$.
\newline

Find the average speed for the whole journey.
\newline

content.content:
A car travels $50\,\text{km}$ at an average speed of $75\,\text{km/h}$.
\newline

Find the time taken.
\newline

Give your answer in minutes.
\newline

Another car travels $47\,\text{km}$, correct to the nearest kilometre.
\newline

The average speed of this car is $75\,\text{km/h}$, correct to the nearest $5\,\text{km/h}$.
\newline

Calculate the lower bound of the time taken.
\newline

Give your answer in minutes.
\newline

A train travels a total of $240\,\text{km}$.
\newline

The train travels for $t$ minutes at an average speed of $100\,\text{km/h}$.
\newline

It then travels for $(t + 60)$ minutes at an average speed of $110\,\text{km/h}$.
\newline

Find the average speed for the whole journey.
\newline

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\item
\begin{subparts}
\item A car travels 50\,km at an average speed of 75\,km/h.

\vgap

Find the time taken.

Give your answer in minutes.

\AnswerLine[][min]
\Marks{2}

\item Another car travels 47\,km, correct to the nearest kilometre.

The average speed of this car is 75\,km/h, correct to the nearest 5\,km/h.

\vgap

Calculate the lower bound of the time taken.

Give your answer in minutes.

\AnswerLine[][min]
\Marks{3}
\end{subparts}

\item A train travels a total of 240\,km.

The train travels for $t$ minutes at an average speed of 100\,km/h.

It then travels for $(t + 60)$ minutes at an average speed of 110\,km/h.

\vgap

Find the average speed for the whole journey.

\AnswerLine[][km/h]
\Marks{6}
\end{parts}

tex:
(a) The diagram shows a solid made from a cylinder and a cone.
\newline

The height of the cylinder is $16\,\text{cm}$ and the height of the cone is $1.5\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

The radius of the cylinder and the base radius of the cone are each $0.35\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

[Diagram]
(i) (b) Calculate the total surface area of the solid.
\newline

[The curved surface area, A, of a cone with radius $r$ and slant height $l$ is $A = \pi rl$]

(c) Calculate the volume of the solid.
\newline

[The volume, V, of a cone with radius $r$ and height $h$ is $V = \dfrac{1}{3}\pi r^2h$]

(d) 10 of the solids are placed in a box in the shape of a cuboid of length $17.5\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

The diagram shows one end of the box.
\newline

[Diagram]
Calculate the volume of the empty space in the box.
\newline

(e) The diagram shows two mathematically similar solids.
\newline

The surface area of the larger solid is $200\,\text{cm}^2$ and the surface area of the smaller solid is $98\,\text{cm}^2$.
\newline

The volume of the larger solid is $450\,\text{cm}^3$.
\newline

[Diagram]
Calculate the volume of the smaller solid.
\newline

tex:
The diagram shows 7 cards.
\newline

[Diagram]
(a) Amir picks a card at random.
\newline

Find the probability that the card shows

(i) (b) the letter H

(c) the letter B.
\newline

(d) Fumika picks one of the 7 cards at random.
\newline

She replaces it and picks a second card at random.
\newline

Find the probability that both cards show the letter I.
\newline

(e) Marcos picks two of the 7 cards at random, without replacement.
\newline

(i) (f) Find the probability that one card shows the letter I and the other card shows the letter N.
\newline

(g) Find the probability that the two cards show different letters.
\newline

(h) Nina picks one of the 7 cards at random without replacement.
\newline

She continues picking cards at random without replacement until she picks a card that shows the letter A.
\newline

The probability that this occurs when she picks the nth card is $\dfrac{4}{21}$.
\newline

Find the value of n.
\newline

content.content:

\newline

the letter H

the letter B.

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

tex:
$y = x^7 - 7x^6$

(a) Find the derivative of $y$ with respect to $x$.
\newline

(b) Find the equation of the tangent to the graph of $y = x^7 - 7x^6$ at the point where $x = -1$.
\newline

Give your answer in the form $y = mx + c$.
\newline

(c) The graph of $y = x^7 - 7x^6$ has two turning points.
\newline

Find the coordinates of these points.
\newline

You must show all your working.
\newline

 (, )

 (, )

content.content:
Find the derivative of $y$ with respect to $x$.
\newline

Find the equation of the tangent to the graph of $y = x^7 - 7x^6$ at the point where $x = -1$.
\newline

Give your answer in the form $y = mx + c$.
\newline

The graph of $y = x^7 - 7x^6$ has two turning points.
\newline

Find the coordinates of these points.
\newline

You must show all your working.
\newline

_rawBody:
$y = x^7 - 7x^6$

\begin{parts}
\item Find the derivative of $y$ with respect to $x$.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}

\item Find the equation of the tangent to the graph of $y = x^7 - 7x^6$ at the point where $x = -1$.

Give your answer in the form $y = mx + c$.

\AnswerLine[$y=$][]
\Marks{4}

\item The graph of $y = x^7 - 7x^6$ has two turning points.

\vgap

Find the coordinates of these points.

You must show all your working.

\hfill (\AnswerLineShort[][], \AnswerLineShort[][])

\vgap

\hfill (\AnswerLineShort[][], \AnswerLineShort[][])
\Marks{5}
\end{parts}
